message:
fix(runtime): reject path traversal in lockstep_harness package.json main resolution (bd-elpy7)

resolve_entry_candidate() accepted untrusted package.json main field values
containing ".." segments, absolute paths, and backslashes, allowing a
malicious package.json to escape the project directory via Path::join().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-1yjq","title":"Remaining clippy warnings: dead code, too-many-args, complex types","description":"After CobaltCat's clippy cleanup (reduced from ~308 to ~49 distinct warnings), the remaining warnings are all non-mechanical: dead code (~30 instances across functions, constants, fields), too-many-arguments (25 instances, needs API refactoring), very-complex-types (5, needs type aliases), module-loaded-multiple-times (4, structural), large-variant/Err-variant (3, enum sizing), must_use without message (1). These require architectural decisions rather than simple fixes.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T21:16:51.475017550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T21:44:01.528043664Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T21:44:01.528020370Z","close_reason":"Fixed all remaining clippy warnings: dead_code (allow annotations on ~30 items), too_many_arguments (allow annotations on 25 functions), type_complexity (type aliases in 3 files), result_large_err (allow annotations), module_inception (allow annotation), must_use (removed redundant attribute), inherent to_string shadow (removed). Only 4 duplicate_mod warnings remain (structural, require module reorganization). Clippy exits 0, all 7400+ tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1ylb","title":"Fresh-eyes audit: randomly selected franken-node module trace and bug-fix pass","description":"Why this exists:
The user requested a fresh-eyes audit pass that starts from randomly selected code files, traces their functionality through related modules, and fixes any concrete bugs or obvious mistakes found along the way.

What this task must do:
- pick non-reserved franken-node modules at random,
- trace execution/data flow through related imports/callers/tests,
- identify at least one concrete, defensible bug if present,
- reserve and fix only the exact file surface needed,
- validate with the lightest appropriate checks, using rch for any cargo-heavy work.

Acceptance criteria:
1. The audited module(s) and traced workflow are documented in Agent Mail / final report.
2. Any fix is scoped narrowly, justified by actual code-path analysis, and revalidated.
3. All heavy cargo work, if any, runs through rch only.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftBeaver","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:46:56.972969120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:04:05.785307686Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T23:04:05.785281898Z","close_reason":"Landed artifact_signing fail-closed verification fix; rch full validation limited by remote timeout/queue behavior","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","fresh-eyes","self-contained"]}
 {"id":"bd-1z3","title":"[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.2 — Compatibility Core

Why This Exists:
Compatibility core as strategic wedge: policy-visible behavior, divergence receipts, L1/L2 lockstep, and spec-first fixture governance.

Task Objective:
Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_2/bd-1z3_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-1z3/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-1z3/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:44.163128134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T09:40:35.689847016Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T09:40:35.689820326Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-2","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z3","depends_on_id":"bd-2kf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:20.220218653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1z5a","title":"[10.17 bug] Replace verifier-economy and replay-capsule trust surrogates with canonical signed capsule verifier","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs` still treats attestation signatures as basically "algorithm label + matching key + non-empty value", `verify_capsule_integrity` still only checks hash presence, and `crates/franken-node/src/connector/verifier_sdk.rs` still accepts any non-empty `signature` field. That means the verifier economy, replay capsules, and public trust scoreboard can present as independently verifiable while still admitting decorative signatures and integrity placeholders.

What this bead must do:
Replace these trust surrogates with one canonical signed-capsule verification kernel shared across verifier-economy and connector surfaces, per the ownership map. The implementation should be stronger than a basic "verify blob signature" path: canonical serialization, detached signature verification, chunked or Merkleized trace commitments for streaming replay, minimal fraud-proof witnesses, and strict binding between claim text, capsule contents, verifier identity, and replay verdict. Verified evidence should be cheap to re-check, impossible to spoof by shape, and rich enough to support public scoreboards without hand-waving.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Attestation signatures and replay capsule integrity in `verifier_economy/mod.rs` and `connector/verifier_sdk.rs` use real canonical serialization and signature/hash verification; presence-only or shape-only acceptance is removed.
2. Capsule integrity binds at least: input state, execution trace commitment, output state, expected-result commitment, verifier identity, claim/version metadata, and issuance timestamp / freshness envelope.
3. Trace commitments are chunked or Merkleized so external verifiers can validate subtraces and emit minimal fraud proofs without replaying everything from scratch.
4. Verifier reputation / public scoreboard updates consume only verified attestations and verified capsules; unverifiable evidence is rejected or explicitly quarantined, never silently scored.
5. The connector verifier SDK and verifier-economy surface share one canonical verification kernel instead of duplicating divergent local checks.
6. Adversarial fixtures cover forged same-length signatures, payload swaps under reused signatures, truncated capsules, cross-claim replay, mismatched trace commitments, and stale/freshness-window violations.
7. Add a checker that fails if signature-present / hash-present shortcuts or equivalent trust surrogates return to replacement-critical verifier paths.
8. Deliver unit tests, integration/E2E capsule replay fixtures, structured logs, benchmark evidence, and machine-readable artifacts proving external replay and trust-score updates are grounded in real verification.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Verifier-economy and connector SDK surfaces share one canonical signed-capsule verifier that binds claim metadata, trace commitments, verifier identity, freshness envelope, and expected-result commitments; signature-present and hash-present shortcuts are forbidden.
2. External verification is user-usable: capsule failures, quarantines, and trust-score decisions emit stable reason codes, fraud-proof bundles, and operator-facing summaries suitable for scoreboards and incident reports.
3. Unit tests cover canonical serialization, chunked or Merkleized subtrace verification, freshness-window handling, payload swaps, truncated capsules, mismatched commitments, and regression checks against decorative signatures.
4. Integration tests exercise connector SDK verification, replay capsule ingestion, scoreboard updates, quarantine decisions, and external re-verification using deterministic fixtures.
5. At least one deterministic operator E2E shell script under tests/e2e/ reproduces successful replay verification, forged capsule rejection, subtrace fraud-proof extraction, quarantine, and scoreboard update logging.
6. Structured logs expose stable CAPSULE_VERIFY_* and VERIFIER_SCORE_* event families with required fields: trace_id, capsule_id, verifier_id, claim_id, commitment_digest, decision, reason_code, and fraud_proof_id.
7. Evidence artifacts under artifacts/replacement_gap/bd-1z5a/ include verification_evidence.json, verification_summary.md, replay_fixture_index.json, and rch benchmark IDs proving external replay verification remains tractable.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FoggySnow","created_at":"2026-03-09T00:10:57.678187558Z","created_by":"RoseMountain","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:04:53.988706334Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-optimization","extreme-optimization","gap-closure","replacement-critical","section-10-17"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a","depends_on_id":"bd-ymfw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T17:48:52.057012920Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":189,"issue_id":"bd-1z5a","author":"FoggySnow","text":"Reopened on 2026-03-12 after stale in-progress ownership. RoseMountain has had no Agent Mail activity since 2026-03-09T05:32:04Z, the bead itself had no owner update since 2026-03-09T17:48:52Z, and the remaining work is still blocking bd-nbwo / bd-3tw7 / bd-3t08.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:04:53Z"}]}

RoseMountain has had no Agent Mail activity since 2026-03-09T05:32:04Z, the bead itself had no owner update since 2026-03-09T17:48:52Z, and the remaining work is still blocking bd-nbwo / bd-3tw7 / bd-3t08.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:04:53Z"},{"id":190,"issue_id":"bd-1z5a","author":"FoggySnow","text":"Revalidated the connector verifier migration-artifact path on 2026-03-12. Focused validation on current HEAD: scripts/check_verifier_sdk.py --json PASS (65/65), pytest tests/test_check_verifier_sdk.py tests/test_check_verifier_sdk_capsule.py PASS (31 passed), rch cargo check -p frankenengine-node --all-targets PASS.  {"id":"bd-1z5a.10","title":"bd-1z5a support: record evidence-pack coherence checker in replacement-gap artifacts","description":"Why this exists:
The new bd-1z5a evidence-pack coherence checker now guards the replacement-gap artifact bundle against silent drift, but the existing replacement-gap pack still does not record that checker or its validation steps. That leaves the pack truthful about older verifier/operator evidence but incomplete about the artifact-level guard that now keeps the pack internally coherent.

What this task must do:
- refresh artifacts/replacement_gap/bd-1z5a/{verification_evidence.json,verification_summary.md,replay_fixture_index.json} so they explicitly include scripts/check_bd_1z5a_evidence_pack.py and its unit tests as first-class replacement-gap verification inputs,
- update the checker/tests as needed so the fixture index and evidence pack stay consistent with the newly recorded artifact-guard lane,
- keep scope limited to the bd-1z5a checker/test/artifact files and avoid the reserved verifier/capsule Rust surfaces.

Acceptance criteria:
1. The replacement-gap evidence pack explicitly records the bd-1z5a coherence checker, its paired tests, and the local validation commands/results.
2. replay_fixture_index.json includes stable entries for the coherence checker lane so downstream reviewers can discover it without scanning mail history.
3. The refreshed checker/tests pass deterministically on the current tree.
4. Scope stays on scripts/tests/artifacts only; no cargo-heavy validation is required.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltHarbor","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:42:51.736372856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:46:01.987873843Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T18:46:01.987849628Z","close_reason":"Refreshed bd-1z5a replacement-gap artifacts to record the evidence-pack coherence checker/test lane, updated required fixture coverage, and revalidated with checker PASS, self-test PASS, unit tests PASS, and py_compile PASS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["replacement-critical","self-contained","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:42:51.736372856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z5a.11","title":"[bd-1z5a] Add signed replay-capsule verification to verifier economy","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs` currently recomputes replay capsule integrity hashes, freshness, and trace commitments, but the `ReplayCapsule` artifact itself still carries no detached verifier signature or signer key binding. That means a capsule can be internally self-consistent yet still be forgeable by any actor who can populate matching fields.

What this task must do:
- add signed replay-capsule payload binding for the verifier-economy capsule artifact,
- verify that signature during registration/access,
- keep the implementation shared with connector verifier helpers rather than inventing a one-off local signature path,
- add focused inline regression tests for tampered signature / tampered payload / wrong signer cases,
- route cargo-heavy validation through rch only if attempted.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Replay capsules in the verifier economy carry verifier key/signature material and are rejected if the signature does not validate over the canonical capsule payload.
2. Tampering with bound capsule fields after signing is rejected even if the integrity hash is recomputed.
3. The signature helper logic lives in shared verifier code, not duplicated ad hoc in verifier_economy.
4. Focused tests cover success, tampered payload, and wrong signer key.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkPond","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:32:21.012877953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T09:49:34.603358056Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T09:49:34.603323602Z","close_reason":"Closeout audit 2026-03-11: acceptance criteria satisfied by current tree. crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs now binds ReplayCapsule signatures to canonical replay_capsule_signature_payload(...) and verifies through cached Ed25519 key checks, with focused regression tests covering tampered signatures, forged claim hash binding, and wrong verifier key. Supporting checker/test evidence: scripts/check_verifier_economy.py --json PASS 152/152 and targeted unittest suites 88 tests OK.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capsule","replay","security","verifier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:32:21.012877953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-e5cz","title":"[16] Output contract: externally replicated high-impact claims","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 16

Task Objective:
Deliver externally replicated high-impact claims.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [16] Output contract: externally replicated high-impact claims are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [16] Output contract: externally replicated high-impact claims are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_16/bd-e5cz/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_16/bd-e5cz/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[16] Output contract: externally replicated high-impact claims" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[16] Output contract: externally replicated high-impact claims" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. At least 3 high-impact claims are identified for external replication: (a) >= 95% compatibility corpus pass rate, (b) >= 10x host-compromise reduction, (c) >= 3x migration velocity improvement.
2. Each claim has a replication kit: (a) precise claim statement with measurement methodology, (b) all data/tools needed to replicate, (c) step-by-step replication instructions, (d) expected results with acceptable variance bounds (within 10%).
3. At least 2 claims have been replicated by independent external parties (different from the project team).
4. Replication results are published alongside original claims with: replicator identity, methodology notes, results, and delta from original.
5. Discrepancies > 10% between original and replicated results trigger investigation and published explanation.
6. Replication kits are tested in CI to ensure they remain functional as the project evolves.
7. Evidence: replication_results.json with per-claim: claim statement, original result, replicator, replicated result, delta, and investigation notes if applicable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:37.303012227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T06:37:46.787400800Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T06:37:46.787375403Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-16","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-e5cz","depends_on_id":"bd-1sgr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:26.813544107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. First safe extension onboarding pathway exists: from 'I have an idea' to 'signed, published, running in production' with minimal friction.
2. Onboarding is automation-first: (a) project scaffold generator ('npx create-franken-extension'), (b) automatic signing setup integrated into publish workflow, (c) automated compatibility and security checks pre-publish, (d) one-command publish to signed registry.

Evidence: extension_onboarding_timing.json with per-step timings for the CI test run.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:36.611525969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T06:45:13.748730813Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T06:45:13.748705316Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-elog","depends_on_id":"bd-31tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:26.428914866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-f2y","title":"[10.8] Implement incident bundle retention and export policy.","description":"## [10.8] Implement incident bundle retention and export policy

### Why This Exists

Section 9I.16 requires classification of control-plane artifacts into retention classes with enforceable policies. Without a formal retention system, incident bundles accumulate without bound (causing storage pressure), or are prematurely deleted (destroying compliance evidence). The operational readiness track (10.8) demands that retention be automated, auditable, and policy-driven — not left to ad-hoc operator cleanup. This bead ensures that critical artifacts survive for compliance and forensics while ephemeral artifacts are cleaned up predictably.

### What It Must Do

Implement a retention policy engine that classifies, stores, and expires control-plane artifacts according to configurable policies:

- **Artifact classification**: Every control-plane artifact is assigned a retention class at creation time. Two base classes: `required` (incident bundles, decision receipts, revocation events, audit logs, epoch transition records, evidence ledger snapshots) and `ephemeral` (health pings, status polls, routine heartbeats, transient cache entries). Classification is determined by artifact type metadata, not by manual tagging.
- **Retention periods**: Required artifacts have configurable minimum retention periods (default: 90 days for incident bundles, 365 days for revocation events, 7 years for audit logs). Ephemeral artifacts have configurable maximum lifetimes (default: 24 hours for health pings, 7 days for status polls). Periods are configurable per deployment via config.
- **Storage enforcement**: Required artifacts must be stored durably (not in-memory or temp directories). The system must verify durability at write time (fsync or equivalent) and raise an alert if durable write fails. Ephemeral artifacts may use volatile storage.
- **Automated expiry**: A retention sweeper runs on a configurable schedule (default: hourly) and removes expired ephemeral artifacts. Required artifacts are never automatically deleted — only moved to a `pending_archive` state after retention period, requiring explicit operator action to delete.
- **Capacity alerts**: When storage utilization crosses configurable thresholds (default: 70% warn, 85% critical), structured alerts are emitted with the largest artifact categories and recommended actions.
- **Export policy**: Incident bundles must be exportable as self-contained archives (tar.gz) containing all related artifacts, metadata, and a manifest. The export format must be versioned and include a cryptographic integrity hash. Exports can target local filesystem, S3-compatible storage, or stdout for piping.
- **Compliance evidence**: Every retention policy decision (keep, expire, archive, export) is logged as an auditable event with timestamp, artifact ID, policy rule applied, and outcome.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. All control-plane artifacts are automatically classified as `required` or `ephemeral` at creation time based on artifact type; no unclassified artifacts exist after one sweep cycle.
2. Retention periods are configurable per artifact class and per deployment; defaults match the specification above.
3. Required artifacts are stored durably with write-time verification; a test simulates durable write failure and confirms alert emission.
4. The retention sweeper removes expired ephemeral artifacts on schedule and never deletes required artifacts automatically.
5. Capacity alerts fire at configurable thresholds with structured output identifying the largest artifact categories.
6. Incident bundle export produces a self-contained versioned archive with integrity hash; re-import of exported bundle passes integrity verification.
7. Every retention decision is logged as an auditable event; the audit trail can be queried by artifact ID or time range.
8. A verification script validates that all current artifacts have valid classifications and no required artifacts are missing or corrupted.

### Key Dependencies

- Artifact persistence layer (from 10.13 chain, retention_policy.rs)
- Evidence ledger infrastructure for storing compliance evidence
- Config system (config.rs) for retention period configuration
- Health gate (health_gate.rs) for capacity alert integration

### Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests for classification logic, expiry calculation, and export format.
- Integration test simulating full lifecycle: create -> classify -> retain -> expire -> export.
- Verification script (`scripts/check_retention_policy.py`) with `--json` and `self_test()`.
- All retention decisions logged at INFO level; capacity alerts at WARN/ERROR.

### Expected Artifacts

- `docs/specs/section_10_8/bd-f2y_contract.md` — retention policy specification
- `scripts/check_retention_policy.py` — verification script
- `tests/test_check_retention_policy.py` — unit tests
- `artifacts/section_10_8/bd-f2y/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_8/bd-f2y/verification_summary.md`","acceptance_criteria":"1. Incident bundles are self-contained archives containing: logs, metrics snapshot, configuration state, error context, and timeline of events for a specific incident window.
2. Retention policy is configurable: minimum retention period, maximum storage budget, and automatic expiry/rotation.
3. Export policy supports at least two formats: compressed archive (tar.gz) for offline analysis and structured JSON stream for ingestion by external SIEM/observability tools.
4. Bundles are integrity-protected: each bundle includes a SHA-256 checksum and an optional signature for chain-of-custody verification.
5. Retention policy enforcement is automated: expired bundles are cleaned up without operator intervention, with a log entry for each deletion.
6. A CLI command (e.g., franken-node incident export <incident-id>) produces the export artifact.
7. Integration test creates an incident bundle, applies retention policy to expire it, and verifies cleanup occurred correctly.","notes":"Plan-space QA addendum (2026-02-20):
1) Preserve full canonical-plan scope; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
2) Completion requires comprehensive verification coverage appropriate to scope: unit tests, integration tests, and E2E scripts/workflows with detailed structured logging (stable event/error codes + trace correlation IDs).
3) Completion requires reproducible evidence artifacts (machine-readable + human-readable) that allow independent replay and root-cause triage.
4) Any implementation PR for this bead must include explicit links to the above test/logging artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:48.026309178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:43:22.412293032Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:43:22.412256805Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-8","self-contained","test-obligations"]}
 {"id":"bd-f5d","title":"[10.9] Build public Node/Bun/franken_node benchmark campaign infrastructure.","description":"## [10.9] Build public Node/Bun/franken_node benchmark campaign infrastructure

### Why This Exists

Section 14 (public benchmark specs) and Section 3.2 #10 (public verifier toolkit) require that franken_node's claims of superior compatibility, performance, and security be backed by reproducible, externally verifiable benchmarks. Without a public benchmark campaign, claims are marketing assertions rather than engineering evidence. The benchmark infrastructure must be rigorous enough that skeptical external parties can reproduce results independently, and comprehensive enough to cover the full competitive surface: correctness, performance, security, and migration velocity.

### What It Must Do

Build end-to-end benchmark campaign infrastructure that enables reproducible, multi-dimensional comparison of Node.js, Bun, and franken_node:

- **Benchmark harness**: A containerized, hermetic benchmark runner that executes benchmark suites against Node.js, Bun, and franken_node under identical conditions. The harness must control: runtime version, OS/kernel version, hardware profile (or normalized cloud instance type), warmup cycles, iteration count, and statistical significance thresholds.
- **Benchmark dimensions** (all required):
  1. **Compatibility correctness**: Pass rate by API family (fs, net, crypto, streams, etc.) and risk band (safe, conditional, unsafe). Measured against the official Node.js test suite and franken_node's extended compatibility matrix.
  2. **Performance**: p50/p95/p99 latency, throughput (req/s), cold start time, memory overhead, and overhead under hardening (trust verification enabled vs disabled). Both micro-benchmarks and macro-benchmarks (realistic workloads).
  3. **Containment/revocation latency**: Time from policy violation detection to containment action, and from revocation issuance to fleet-wide propagation.
  4. **Replay determinism**: Percentage of operations that produce bit-identical results under replay. Measured across runtime versions.
  5. **Adversarial resilience**: Pass rate against adversarial test suite (malicious extensions, credential exfiltration, policy evasion).
  6. **Migration speed/failure-rate**: Time and success rate for migrating representative Node.js projects to franken_node.
- **Scoring formulas**: Each dimension has a published scoring formula that converts raw measurements into a normalized score (0-100). Formulas are versioned and published alongside results so that score changes are attributable to measurement changes, not formula changes.
- **Dataset catalog**: Benchmark workloads and datasets are versioned, published, and downloadable. No benchmark depends on private data.
- **Result publishing**: Results are published as structured JSON with full provenance (harness version, runtime versions, hardware profile, raw measurements, computed scores). Results include cryptographic hashes for integrity verification.
- **External reproducibility**: A "reproduce this benchmark" script that external parties can run on their own infrastructure. The script downloads the harness, datasets, and runtime versions, executes the suite, and produces a comparison report.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. Benchmark harness runs in a hermetic container with pinned runtime versions and produces deterministic results (variance < 5% across runs on identical hardware).
2. All six benchmark dimensions are implemented with at least one benchmark suite each.
3. Scoring formulas are documented, versioned, and published alongside results.
4. Benchmark datasets and workloads are publicly downloadable with integrity hashes.
5. Results are published as structured JSON with full provenance metadata and integrity verification.
6. An external reproducibility script enables independent parties to reproduce benchmarks and verify published results.
7. A CI integration runs a subset of benchmarks (smoke suite) on every release and flags performance regressions exceeding configurable thresholds.
8. Benchmark infrastructure documentation explains how to add new dimensions, workloads, and scoring formulas.

### Key Dependencies

- Compatibility matrix from 10.2 (compatibility core) for correctness benchmarks
- Adversarial test suite (bd-9is) for resilience benchmarks
- Migration pipeline (bd-1e0) for migration speed benchmarks
- Trust verification infrastructure for containment/revocation latency benchmarks

### Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests for scoring formula calculations and result serialization.
- Integration tests that run the full harness against a mock runtime and verify output format.
- Verification script (`scripts/check_benchmark_infra.py`) with `--json` and `self_test()`.
- Benchmark execution logged at INFO with per-suite timing; anomalies logged at WARN.

### Expected Artifacts

- `docs/specs/section_10_9/bd-f5d_contract.md` — benchmark specification and scoring formulas
- `scripts/check_benchmark_infra.py` — verification script
- `tests/test_check_benchmark_infra.py` — unit tests
- `fixtures/benchmarks/` — benchmark suite definitions and datasets
- `artifacts/section_10_9/bd-f5d/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_9/bd-f5d/verification_summary.md`","acceptance_criteria":"1. Benchmark campaign compares Node.js, Bun, and franken_node across at least 10 real-world workloads: HTTP server throughput, module loading, cold start, JSON processing, file I/O, child process spawning, stream throughput, crypto operations, URL parsing, and compatibility-shim overhead.
2. Campaign infrastructure produces reproducible results: all benchmarks run in containerized environments with pinned runtime versions and identical hardware profiles.
3. Results are published as a structured JSON dataset with per-benchmark, per-runtime mean/median/p95/p99 latency and throughput numbers.
4. Campaign includes a comparative visualization generator (HTML or Markdown report) with charts and tables suitable for public consumption.
5. Per Section 3 category-defining targets: report highlights where franken_node achieves >= 95% Node.js compatibility, >= 3x migration velocity, or >= 10x compromise reduction.
6. Campaign is re-runnable on new releases with a single command (scripts/run_benchmark_campaign.sh) and automatically diffs against previous results.
7. Methodology document explains statistical rigor: number of iterations, warm-up policy, outlier handling, and confidence intervals.","notes":"Plan-space QA addendum (2026-02-20):
1) Preserve full canonical-plan scope; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
2) Completion requires comprehensive verification coverage appropriate to scope: unit tests, integration tests, and E2E scripts/workflows with detailed structured logging (stable event/error codes + trace correlation IDs).
3) Completion requires reproducible evidence artifacts (machine-readable + human-readable) that allow independent replay and root-cause triage.
4) Any implementation PR for this bead must include explicit links to the above test/logging artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightReef","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:48.287999303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T01:16:13.847385863Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T01:16:13.847347932Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-9","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-f5d","depends_on_id":"bd-1xg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:37.840224751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-f5d","depends_on_id":"bd-20a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:37.895375020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-f5d","depends_on_id":"bd-229","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:37.943889570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-f5d","depends_on_id":"bd-3il","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:37.988491682Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-f5d","depends_on_id":"bd-cda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:38.032583014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
